name: Passover Dark Chocolate Nut Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Very finely chopped almonds or pecans
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 10 to 15
DIRECTIONS: Set a large mixing bowl in a sink filled with warm water. Separate the eggs (you should have about 1 1/2 cups whites). Pour the whites into the warm mixing bowl and place yolks in a smaller mixing bowl (or let separated eggs stand, covered, at room temperature for 30 minutes). Meanwhile, in a small, heavy saucepan, partially melt unsweetened chocolate and semisweet chocolate pieces over low heat. Turn off heat and stir (chocolate will continue to melt).
Cut a waxed paper liner for the bottom of a 10-inch tube pan, cutting it half an inch wider than the pan and the center hole slightly larger than the tube. Grease the pan thoroughly. Place the liner in the pan and grease it.
Beat egg yolks with an electric mixer on high speed for about 4 minutes or till they are thick and lemon colored. Add the 2/3 cup sugar, beating till very thick. Stir in the melted chocolate and chopped nuts.
Thoroughly wash and dry beaters. Beat egg whites with vanilla and almond extract until foamy. Gradually add the 1/4 cup sugar to the egg whites, beating till soft peaks form. Lightly fold about 2 cups of the beaten egg whites into the yolk mixture. Add the remaining egg white mixture and gently fold together.
Pour batter into the prepared pan. Bake at 350°F for about 40 minutes or till the top springs back when lightly touched. Run a knife around the edge of the pan and center to loosen. Cool in the pan for 1 hour. Invert onto a cake plate. Remove the waxed paper.
Prepare glaze; spread over the top and sides of the cake.
Dark Chocolate Glaze: In a small heavy saucepan or skillet, melt chocolate over low heat. Remove from heat and stir in butter or margarine. In a small bowl, dissolve instant coffee in hot water. Stir in the sour cream and maple flavoring. Add the sour cream mixture to the chocolate, stirring till smooth and shiny. Makes 3/4 cup.
